title: Normalized Feed Domain, Content Variants, and Provenance · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds loss-aware normalized feed and entry model from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of loss-aware normalized feed and entry model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Convert format-specific transport values into validated feed, entry, person, link, category, content, and provenance domain types.
Choose entry identity from stable Atom ID, RSS GUID semantics, canonical link, or scoped content fingerprint with explicit precedence.
Preserve plain text, escaped HTML, XHTML, external content, summary, and missing content as distinct bounded states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Transport Domain Separation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 8147 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile format-specific source values with normalized identity, content kinds, and loss evidence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Transport Domain Separation.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Go Loss Report
steps.instruction:
